channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: One of the Thames water covers has broken, 15th May
item.description:
The cover for the water access point has broken. D now there’s a hole that could trip someone over.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 230, High Street, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: There is still a pot hole on the road there were two that needed fixing Near 57 / 55 Cambridge Road, 15th May
item.description:
There is still a pot hole on the road there were two that needed fixing The pothole is very large yesterday you came and resolved one pothole when there was visibly too large potholes. The potholed is big and sunken and many cars often sinking into this pothole when passing our street. It is also causing parking issues as any cars parked near this pothole becomes covered in dirty muddy water and cars that are driving down the road are often not noticing and sliding dangerously over these potholes. As the pothole is so large, this reduces spaces that we can park on the road safely. I previously reported this pothole with images and stated that there were two large potholes on the road. please can you send someone out to repair the last remaining pothole thank you.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cambridge Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 15th May
item.description:
Fly tipping
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Cole Close, Thamesmead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 15th May
item.description:
Fly tipping
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Cole Close, Thamesmead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bench damaged further, 15th May
item.description:
The bench previously reported as damaged has been damaged further by a group of teenage boys who have pulled the wooden planks out and were waving them about. They have left them in a pile which is a trip hazard
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 4-5, Market Place, Bexley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Young Tree Needs Urgent Support, 15th May
item.description:
We were in an urban tree festival walk when we saw that the stake had broken off, and tree was almost falling to one side. Paul's Scarlett hawthorn is a wonderful tree to plant as a street tree. Please save it as a matter of urgency Thank you

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1-15, Bradley Close, Barnsbury, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Motorbikes/delivery bikes and lorries turning out of industrial estate without due care, 15th May
item.description:
Raised crossing should signal that pedestrians take priority
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Verney Road, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Cycle lane has been completed for months but not opened?, 15th May
item.description:
The cycle lane looks complete in areas but it hasn't been opened. The signals at the junction are set up for cycle crossing through the bike lane but despite it looking finished it is still walled off. What are we waiting for?
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ilderton Road, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Island crossing should be zebra crossing, 15th May
item.description:
Cars do not stop for people trying to cross here. This is part of a major cycle route so potentially should have separate crossing lane for cyclists too.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 158, Trundley's Road, New Cross Gate, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Cars turning too fast without seeing pedestrians due to road design, 15th May
item.description:
Cars turning left onto Galleywall Road from Rotherhithe New Road do so at speed and are unable to see pedestrians properly. There is a bus stop here that pedestrians cross over from once getting off a bus. This junction needs traffic calming measures e.g. narrower turn, different raised road surface (like brick paving) that shows this isn't a main road like Rotherhithe New Road, or a zebra crossing.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Galleywall Road, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Mattress dumped on footpath, 15th May
item.description:
Block 6 Devon Mansions

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tooley Street, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pavement impassable due to residents hedge, 15th May
item.description:
The foliage from 59 York way has grown across the pap pavement to such an extent that it is completely impassable. Something must be done. No doubt Barnet Council having a big x marks a spot on a map and the address will come back and say they're unable to find it, despite having full legal powers to write a letter to the resident to instruct them to cut it back and when they fail to do so all that Barnet Council have the full legal power to cut it off.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): York Way, Brunswick Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Food dumped down alleyway, 15th May
item.description:
Food has been dumped again down the alleyway behind 14-19 old school close. Everyone on old school close has received a letter from Accent housing association reminding them it is an offence. I have cleared it up as it was discovered after you have closed. It is dangerous to cats and therefore couldn't be left. It could be thrown over a wall from other properties gardens. Can it be investigated.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Old School Close, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 9301631, 15th May
item.description:
The potholes have not been repaired, I fell off my bike and could of been injured.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 20, King Henry's Road, Chalk Farm, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Motorbikes in the Zipwire again, 15th May
item.description:
Fast and noisy motor bikes in the park- ongoing antisocial behaviour with no consequenes
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Magpie Hall Lane, Bromley Common, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Near bus stop, 15th May
item.description:
Keeps having rubbish left on my wall or put into my garden you need to put a bin at bus stop so rubbish it disgusting this will attract all types animals and it my front garden getting effect too
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Mottingham Road, Coldharbour, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Timeplate sprayed on Gairloch Road, 15th May
item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 20, Gairloch Road, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Someone dumped a fridge outside my door, 15th May
item.description:
Someone has dumped a fridge outside my front door Outside 26 aberdour street

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Aberdour Street, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Out Cavendish Mansions on Rosbery Ave, 15th May
item.description:
Double kerb blips have faded and vans and cars are parking here and the parking violation is not being enforced.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rosebery Avenue, Holborn, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti, 15th May
item.description:
I have reported this issue previously and website keeps removing. Graffiti is graffiti and needs to be removed. Especially with all the attacks of late. It is not appropriate and can be deemed offensive.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Turkey Street, Enfield
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Offensive up to 2 metres off the ground
